response:
Section 1  
To implement the function, we need to process the input text and count the occurrences of the specified word, ignoring case sensitivity. We will use Python's string methods to handle the case insensitivity and punctuation.

Section 2  
We will split the text into words, convert each word to lowercase, and compare it with the target word. This ensures that the count is case-insensitive. We will also handle any punctuation that may be present in the text.

Section 3  
The function will iterate through each word in the text, check if it matches the target word in lowercase, and increment a counter each time a match is found. This approach ensures that we count all occurrences of the word, regardless of case or punctuation.

Section 4  
Finally, we will return the count as an integer. We will also ensure that the function adheres to the constraints, such as handling edge cases like empty text or non-existent words. The function will be efficient and straightforward, making it easy to understand and use.

P.S.: Your response must have 4 sections.
